reasoning.typography: Elegant serif display (likely Domaine) paired with clean sans-serif creates sophistication. Not the AI stencil, but serif-for-dev-tools is becoming a micro-trend. Strong character.
reasoning.hierarchy: Exceptional clarity—massive headline dominates, single message, perfect visual weight distribution. The 'Email for developers' is doing all the work it needs to.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Clean dev tool aesthetic with good execution, but swap 'Resend' for 'Courier' or 'Postmark' and the visual system holds. Logo wall is generic trust signaling. Formula over fingerprint.
reasoning.color: Disciplined near-monochrome palette, dark mode done well. Accents are restrained. No decorative color pollution. Professional and confident.
reasoning.density: Confident use of negative space—not afraid to let the hero breathe. Massive padding around core message shows restraint. Logo grid has proper spacing. Premium density balance.
reasoning.voice: Structure is pure SaaS stencil: Hero + Social Proof Logos + CTA. The 'Email for developers' positioning is sharp, but the IA is completely interchangeable with any dev tool. No structural surprise.

winning_patterns: Massive typographic hierarchy with serif display creates immediate gravitas
Confident negative space usage—not filling every pixel
Restrained monochrome palette executed with discipline
Sharp positioning copy ('reach humans instead of spam folders')
Logo wall properly integrated without visual chaos
anti_patterns: Complete SaaS stencil architecture: hero + logos + CTA
Generic logo wall trust signaling (Warner Bros to Paper—every dev tool does this)
No structural surprise or navigation curiosity—pure conversion funnel
Dark mode is trendy but not distinctive for this category anymore
Could swap the product name and have identical visual system work
